analyzation.transcript: We ditched the bottle at 12 months and here's how we did it. As a speech language pathologist, I was really mindful about the cup we chose. A lot of leak proof cups have really strong valves and you end up having to bite the straw to get the milk to come out and that's not the drinking pattern we want to reinforce. That's why we love the Grosmimi straw cup. At first, I took the straw out so he could tip it back and drink it like it was a bottle. Once he was familiar with it, I put the straw back in, and he was able to drink it just like normal from a straw cup. Comment milk and I'll send you a link to my favorite cup.

Baby Registry Series Part 2: Feeding Essentials 🍼✨

If you’re building your baby registry and wondering what you actually need for feeding baby, here’s everything I’d register for as a first time mom:

• @Evenflo Baby (great anti-colic option for newborns)
• Shimi formula bottles for easy prep + on-the-go feeding
• Boon drying rack for bottle organization
• Willow Go wearable breast pumps (hands-free pumping essential) 
• Mushie splat mat for mess-free feeding
• Stokke high chair (a long-term investment piece)
• electric bottle scrubber to save time during the newborn stage
• Elk & Friends plates for feeding solids 
• silicone bibs with catchers 
• Grosmimi Sippy Cups 
• Spectra Breast Pump

These are my newborn feeding must haves whether you’re breastfeeding, formula feeding, or combo feeding. I’m all about practical baby registry picks that make postpartum life easier.

Save this for your registry planning 🤍

Follow along for more realistic baby registry must haves + mom content.

analyzation.transcript: Welcome back to part two of my series where I help you build your baby registry category by category. For reference, I'm a first time mom to an 11 month old and here are the items that I recommend for your feeding category. Even Flo bottles. They have a much different sizes. These are the standard neck ones. There's not a ton of parts, so they're easy to clean. Love. If you're a mom who plans on formula feeding or switching to formula at some point, it's really awesome to have these Shimi bottles on hand. I use these specifically like when we're going out to dinner or running around or traveling on the plane. It's nice because you fill this top portion with formula and then it screws off, you dump it into the water and then you shake it. You never know. I love these. For wearable breast pumps, I had the Willow Go's and I absolutely love them. I can control them from my phone. Um they were super comfortable. They came with different flange sizes and I just love them. Having a really good bottle scrubber. I have an electric one from Amazon. It's awesome if you do plan to bottle feed or formula feed, having something that can help you wash bottles is necessary. I did the Boon Trove drying rack for bottles as well. That's just easy to use. You can take it apart, put it in the dishwasher if you ever need to wash it. We also went with the Stoke high chair. Um it's been really great so far. I love the height of it because it's like the perfect length for our or the perfect height for our kitchen table where we can just pull them up and he's eating literally eating right next to us. Um it's also great because it grows with them. A splat mat for under your high chair. This is something that I didn't register for but bought afterwards. I use the mushy splat mat. I'm gonna go ahead and list out a few more items in the caption as well, but these are just the ones that I really want to highlight. Come back for part three.

analyzation.transcript: Hàn Quốc đợt này của tôi đúng là bốc trúng được xịt đụ luôn, vừa được ngắm cảnh lá vàng mà lại còn vừa được nhìn thấy tuyết đầu mùa nữa. Mỗi tội là trời lạnh quá nên là cóng hết cả tay đấy các bà ạ. Mặc dù là đã từng ở Hàn mấy năm, thế nhưng mà đến lúc quay lại á vẫn cảm thấy là cái mùa đông ở bên này. Trời ơi sao mà nó rét kinh khủng khiếp. Có những ngày thời tiết âm tận gần 10 độ, không chỉ lạnh đâu mà còn khô nữa cơ. Coi như là từ mặt cho đến người cứ khô nứt toác cả ra, nếu như mà không bôi kem á thì là đêm sẽ khỏi ngủ vì bị ngứa ấy. May là trước khi đi Hàn cũng đã phải làm tư tưởng với Thị Miu là cho mẹ mượn cái lọ kem dưỡng da Cha and Mom để mang theo rồi đấy. Thị Miu thì được cái là khuôn mặt không giống mẹ tí gì nhưng mà trộm vía cái làn da thì y xì đúc mẹ luôn, cứ thời tiết lạnh hay khô một cái là biết mặt nhau liền. Kem dưỡng ẩm của Cha and Mom thì có hai loại, một loại là loại dưỡng chuyên sâu 72 giờ và một loại nữa là lotion dưỡng thể, nó sẽ mỏng nhẹ hơn và Miu cũng đang dùng hằng ngày. Trong đây thì sẽ có chiết xuất từ quả kỷ tử Hàn Quốc, nước lá nha đam Jeju, ngoài ra thì có chiết xuất Real Island, bơ hạt mỡ, dầu Jojoba. Trong những điểm đặc biệt của cái thương hiệu Cha and Mom này, đó chính là ứng dụng công nghệ lợi khuẩn độc quyền Phytoseline sẽ giúp hỗ trợ tạo ra một hàng rào độ ẩm để bảo vệ da, giúp làm dịu và làm tăng cường tính đàn hồi trên da. Nếu như mà da khô vừa phải thì các bà có thể dùng dạng lotion, còn nếu như mà da rất khô, dễ bong tróc thì các bà có thể dùng dạng cream, dưỡng ẩm lên đến 72 giờ giống như là tôi nè. Ngoài cái tác dụng là dưỡng ẩm, giảm ngứa cho da thì kem của Cha and Mom còn hỗ trợ trong các cái vấn đề và tình trạng về da. Ví dụ như là tràm sữa, mụn, phát ban hay là viêm da cơ địa. Mấy cái này thì tôi sẽ chia sẻ rõ hơn cho các bà ở những clip sau nha. Mấy hôm ở bên Hàn trời vừa lạnh vừa khô nên là lúc nào trong túi của tôi cũng có một cái tuýp nhỏ của Cha and Mom như thế này nè. Nhờ vậy mà một cái đứa thích nghịch tuyết như tôi, mặc dù là tay bị cóng xíu thôi nhưng mà lúc nào cũng ẩm mịn và không bị khô nứt nẻ. Công nhận đi Hàn thì cảnh đẹp thật, thích thật đấy các bà ạ, thế nhưng mà lạnh quá vẫn là đi về với con gái yêu là hợp lý hơn đấy các bà ơi.

analyzation.transcript: I've been on the hunt for the perfect sippy cup to help my son transition from bottles, now that he's one. These are the Grosmimi straw cups that are also available with a flip-top lid. Filling it with milk or water is super easy and the soft silicone straw is gentle on his gums. Plus, the rotating handle protects his little wrists while he drinks. The cross-cut design makes it completely leak-proof, even if you shake it or it falls. I love the clear measurements on the side that make it so simple to track how much he's drinking. It's also super easy to take apart and put back together. The straw and lid pop off in seconds for cleaning. They're medical grade, durable, scratch-resistant, BPA-free, and odor resistant. He can drink all by himself now and the straw is just the right size for him. It's safe to say the Grosmimi straw cup has made the switch from bottle to sippy cup super simple.

analyzation.transcript: I was today years old when I realized I was accidentally feeding my baby microplastics. I didn't realize how scratched up our old cups were until I really looked at them and that honestly freaked me out. I'm glad I switched to a PPSU cup. It's basically bulletproof plastic. You can boil it to sterilize it without it breaking down and it won't get those gross cloudy scratches where bacteria likes to hide. And it doesn't leak, which as a mom, matters a lot. It's just one of those swaps that makes me feel way better about what my baby's actually using and taking every single day. Not to mention all the adorable designs available. Make sure to check out the link in my bio to check them out for yourself.

analyzation.transcript: This is a very realistic what my 14-month-old eats for breakfast. First, he started with one egg and shredded cheese. I like to make it into an omelet. Next is some Polish sausage that we get from Costco, and he's really been liking that lately. Lastly, I just cut him up a mandarin orange. He always loves his fruit. We've also been obsessed with using the Gross Mimi straw cups lately, which he always drinks his water out of. And that's it. He did pretty good today. He never quite finishes all of his eggs, but he did finish the sausage and oranges. And as you can see, he was very distracted by me filming him while he was eating.

The Grosmimi straw cup is spill resistant, easy to use, and perfect for toddlers learning to drink independently. It's made with PPSU material, which is known for being durable and producing less microplastic shedding compared to some alternatives, something that really matters to me. One of my favorite parts is that they also offer a stainless steel option. It gives them that sense of independence without the constant mess, which has made everyday things like playtime and leaving the house so much easier. This has quickly become our go-to cup and it's one of the few toddler products that truly works for us.

analyzation.transcript: Do you know what your baby drinks from everyday? With my older kids, we went through so many cups. They scratched so easily. I wanted something that holds up over time. That's when I found a PPSU sippy cup. PPSU is a medical-grade material used in hospitals. It's durable, heat-resistant, and dishwasher-safe. No spills, even when they tip it. One less thing to worry about, because kids can hold and tilt it comfortably, they can drink to the very last drop. After using it with my older child, I chose the same one again for my youngest. I'm glad I figured this out early. Now I feel better about what my kids use every day.

Quà tặng thì cực kỳ ngập tràn sự chỉn chu đến từ nhãn hàng, đến từ thương hiệu. Điều khiển mình ấn tượng hơn cả chính là con người trong sự kiện, các anh chị đại diện nhãn hàng thì vô cùng thân thiện gần gũi, nói chuyện thì rất thiện dương và tạo cảm giác thoải mái cho khách mời. Đội ngũ nhân viên thì hỗ trợ cực kỳ nhiệt tình luôn có mặt khi mà các mẹ cần nhẹ nhàng và chuyên nghiệp không hề có cảm giác hình thức hay xa cách. Phần chia sẻ sản phẩm mới cũng là một điểm rất cộng. Khi mà chia sẻ xong thì các mẹ sẽ được cùng nhau nâng ly này, được cùng nhau chia sẻ và quà mang về thì rất là thiết thực luôn. Và mình ấy thì hi vọng trong thời gian sắp tới mình có thêm nhiều cơ hội được đồng hành lắng nghe và chia sẻ những giá trị tốt đẹp mà Cha&Mom đang hướng tới. Cảm ơn tất cả các mẹ.

analyzation.transcript: I was tired of my toddler's cups growing mildew, spilling, or not holding enough water. So I did a little bit of research and found these Grosmimi cups right here and they are our new favorite cups. They have a little handle that moves around so as the water shifts, they can move the handle and get every little last drop in this cup. It holds up to 10 ounces. It does not have a lot of parts, so clean up is very easy and you can throw them in the dishwasher because they're dishwasher safe. And no, they do not spill. They're designed to stop water from coming out. It's hard to see, I don't know if you can tell, but there's like a little stopper right about here inside of the straw. So when they're drinking out of it, they get plenty of water, but if they tip it upside down, nothing comes out. We love this so much that we have it in multiple different colors, but they sell out all the time and very fast. In fact, we waited for this one for the last two months and they just came back in stock, so I'll drop the link right here so that you can grab yours hopefully before they run out again.

analyzation.transcript: As a mom, I was shocked to find out I could be feeding my baby 1.5 million pieces of microplastics. Studies show that PP plastics easily shed microplastics, which can affect hormones, cause gut issues or skin rashes in babies. So I looked for a safer option. That's when I found PPSU, a medical grade material used in hospitals that's BPA free, heat resistant, and incredibly durable. It can handle hot liquids, constant washing, sterilizing, and even the dishwasher without breaking down or releasing anything harmful. And if you're in the toddler stage like me, you know things get dropped daily. This is made for that real life wear and tear. My personal favorite is Growmimi. I love that it doesn't absorb stains, no lingering milk smells, no discoloration. It's made to very strict Korean quality standards. And there's a reason it's the number one cup brand in Korea. It doesn't have to be Growmimi, but please make sure it's a PPSU cup to give yourself a piece of mind knowing it's safe, high quality, and truly made to last.
analyzation.caption: I was feeding my baby plastic all along...
analyzation.video_summary: A mom shares her discovery of microplastics in baby cups and introduces PPSU cups as a safer alternative, highlighting the Growmimi brand for its quality and durability.

That's when I found a PPSU sippy cup. PPSU is a medical-grade material used in hospitals. It's durable, heat-resistant, and dishwasher-safe. No spills, even when they tip it. One less thing to worry about, because kids can hold and tilt it comfortably, they can drink to the very last drop. After using it with my older child, I chose the same one again for my youngest. I'm glad I figured this out early. Now I feel better about what my kids use every day.

analyzation.transcript: As a mom, I was shocked to find out I could be feeding my baby 1.5 million pieces of microplastics. Studies show that PP plastics easily shed microplastics, which can affect hormones, cause gut issues or skin rashes in babies. So I looked for a safer option. That's when I found PPSU, a medical grade material used in hospitals that's BPA free, heat resistant, and incredibly durable. It can handle hot liquids, constant washing, sterilizing, and even the dishwasher without breaking down or releasing anything harmful. And if you're in the toddler stage like me, you know things get dropped daily. This is made for that real life wear and tear. My personal favorite is Growmimi. I love that it doesn't absorb stains, no lingering milk smells, no discoloration. It's made to very strict Korean quality standards. And there's a reason it's the number one cup brand in Korea. It doesn't have to be Growmimi, but please make sure it's a PPSU cup to give yourself a piece of mind knowing it's safe, high quality, and truly made to last.
analyzation.caption: I was feeding my baby plastic all along...

Now, he's still taking four bottles a day, one when he wakes up, one after each nap, and then one a little bit before bed. About two weeks ago, we tried to start this process, and then our family came down with the stomach flu, so we just held off. But we tried with this cup, he drank about an ounce and a half, and then when we tried with this one, he took one sip and was over it. We're going to try this cup today. He loves this cup, he drinks his water out of it. He carries this around, so he's familiar with this cup, so I think it might go over a little bit better. And what I'm going to actually do is I'm going to take the straw out, and he can kind of drink it tipped back, almost like it's a bottle. Over the last few weeks, we've been warming his bottle up less and less as time goes on, just so he would get used to drinking cold milk. However, he still definitely prefers it warm, so for today, I'm going to warm up his milk like he used to drink it. I'm also going to keep him in the nursery in his sleep sack in his little warm cozy environment. It was supposed to be a low pressure practice moment, but he actually shocked me and he drank all four ounces from the straw cup.

analyzation.transcript: When the twins were newborns, they developed eczema and baby acne. And seeing that made me realize just how delicate their skin really is. And I know that baby acne is mostly caused by hormones passed from mom to birth, but it's also caused by environmental factors as well. And as an esthetician and a twin mom, that experience made me extremely picky. I knew I wanted only unscented products, the most gentle ingredients, and formulas that actually support a healthy skin barrier. So I spent a long time researching and comparing baby skincare before I felt comfortable committing to anything. And that's when I found Cha and Mom. This is a brand crafted by Cha Hospital. It's Korea's number one OBGYN center with over 60 years of expertise caring for moms and babies. What really stood out to me is their phyto-saling name, which helps support the skin in a way similar to the natural microbiome babies receive at birth from their moms. The P. S. lotion is now part of our daily bath routine, and the P. S. cream is what I reach for when their skin needs extra comfort. And as a mom and a skincare specialist, this is the one I can really trust for my boys.

extraction.content:
This is something we explain to almost every new family.
 

 During birth, babies get what some people call a “probiotic shower”, a first coating of their parent’s beneficial bacteria.
 

 Then, suddenly, their skin has to learn a whole new environment.
 Newborn skin is thinner, more sensitive, and still learning how to protect itself.
 

 In the first weeks, the goal isn’t to fix or treat skin.
 It’s to protect the barrier and not overwhelm it.
 

 That’s why we guide families toward simple routines and gentle products designed for newborn skin, including lotions like CHA&MOM that we use in real postpartum homes.
 

 If you’re unsure what’s normal, you’re not doing anything wrong.
 

 Most of the time, your baby’s skin just needs time. ❤️

The Meaning of “Onzenna”⁠
 ⁠
 Onzenna comes from the Korean word 언제나, meaning “Always.”⁠
 ⁠
 Because motherhood has no off switch. It isn’t a 9-to-5 role, and it doesn’t pause when the baby sleeps or when the years pass. Soon, Zezebaebae will become Onzenna.⁠
 ⁠
 Same heart. Deeper mission.⁠
 ⁠
 We’re rethinking how motherhood is supported — with less noise, and more intention.⁠
 ⁠
 The full story begins soon.⁠
 ⁠
 Onzenna. Always with you🤍⁠
 ⁠

analyzation.transcript: I just got this cup in yesterday from Grosmimi and I am ecstatic that I'm partnering with them because if you need proof, we love Grosmimi in this house. These were all bought with my hard earned money. Ever since my daughter was six months old, I started her on a straw cup because I did not want her to be using a sippy cup and then have to transition her from that to a straw. So once I wanted to to transition her to a cup in general, it was a straw cup and the Grosmimi is the only one I had any luck with. So we have several of these. I think there's even two more in the dishwasher and there's another upstairs. So we have lots of them already. They were great and I I'm convinced it's why she so easily took to a straw. And then I just got this one in, it's metal. It came with this silicone protector on the bottom to keep it from damaging surfaces and it's one of the flip tops. So it's great to hold, this spins around, so if you know, if the straw is not touching the liquid inside. And there's the straw. And then it also, they also sent me this. It's a flip top with a sip top lid because my daughter's 18 months now and I have to say one thing I have slacked on is teaching her how to drink from a cup. So I'm really excited about this because it's a cup that she's familiar with, but then this type of lid which has the holes so the liquid will come out, but it's not too big so which she doesn't spill everything all over her. Um, I think this will be really great teaching her how to drink. It also included this little handle so you can take take this handle off and put this on. And she can carry it around, like how cute. She already was so excited about this, she was putting it on like it was her little purse. So cute. We love, love, love Grosmimi in this house. I cannot sing their praises enough. Um, and all of their lids are interchangeable. Any of these on any of the cups and they're all interchangeable. So very excited. Thank you so much for sending this Grosmimi. Definitely, definitely, definitely check them out.

analyzation.transcript: เอาจริงนะ ตอนนี้แม่อินกับขวดน้ำหัดดื่มของน้องฮานะมาก วันนี้แม่เลยสั่งมาลองอีกหนึ่งแบรนด์ค่ะ เป็นขวดน้ำดูดจากเกาหลี โกรมีมี่ บอกเลยว่าโดนตกตั้งแต่ดีไซน์ มีหลายลายมาก น่ารักทุกลาย แต่ไม่ได้มีดีแค่ น่ารักนะคะ ฟังก์ชันเขาก็ดีค่ะ อย่างแรกที่แม่ชอบที่สุดเลยนะคะ ก็คือเรื่องหลอดดูด ปลายหลอดเขาเป็นรูปกากบาท ทำให้น้ำไม่พุ่ง ไม่ไหล ไม่หก ช่วยลดการสําลัก แม่สบายใจมาก โดยเฉพาะน้องที่เพิ่งฝึกดูด และเขายังเป็นหลอดลูกตุ้มด้วยนะ จะดูดมุมไหนก็ได้ น้องไม่ต้องพยายามมาก ส่วนตัววัสดุค่ะ ก็เป็น PPSU ปลอดภัย ไม่มี BPA แม่ใช้แล้วอุ่นใจ ฝาเป็นแบบวันทัช เปิดง่าย โดยใช้มือเดียว แล้วก็มีที่จับนะคะ หมุนได้ 360 องศาเลย น้องถือเองได้ ไม่ฝืนข้อมือ แม่ๆ บ้านไหนกำลังมองหาขวดน้ำหัดดูดดีๆ นะคะ แม่ขอแนะนำเลยค่ะ โกรมีมี่ บ้านนี้ใช้แล้ว ชอบจริง

One mistake a lot of parents make when starting solids is their baby not drinking enough water.
 

 Water plays a big role in helping prevent constipation, but it can be hard to tell how much they’re actually getting.
 

 That’s why we love using a clear straw cup — being able to see her water intake gives me so much peace of mind during meals. The soft, flexible straw is perfect for little mouths, and clearly… it’s durable enough for real life 🤍
 

 Comment cup and I’ll send you the link 🤍

 This video breaks down what actually matters when choosing a baby bottle, using the Grosmimi PPSU bottle as an example.

analyzation.transcript: Most bottle issues I see aren't actually about brand, they're about design. As a nurse practitioner and a mom, there are three things I always look for in a baby bottle. First is airflow, airflow matters because it supports smoother, more comfortable feeding. It's not just about the nipple shape. Second is material, medical grade PPSU is lightweight, durable, BPA free, and designed to handle common sterilization methods. And third is having a system that grows with your baby. The Grosmimi PPSU bottle system is designed to use the same core bottle across stages, which helps reduce clutter and unnecessary replacements over time. When a bottle checks airflow, material, and adaptability, it just makes sense for real life feeding.

We particularly love the food grade Grosmimi stainless steel straw cup as it not only keeps her water cold or milk cold for hours, but it's also built durable. It's designed with markings on the inside to ensure I'm able to keep track of how much baby is drinking. It is completely lead free with a spout material that resembles bottle nipples, so it would feel familiar and easy to adapt to. The flip top promotes independence with just a push of a button, and the easy closing cap ensures the straw remains free from contamination. It is so easy for baby to walk around with it with its 360 degree handle, and since it's leak free, I don't worry about spills at all.

Plus the rotating handle protects his little wrist while he drinks. The cross-cut design makes it completely leak-proof even if you shake it or it falls. I love the clear measurements on the side that make it so simple to track how much he's drinking. It's also super easy to take apart and put back together. He can drink all by himself now and the straw is just the right size for us. It's safe to say the Grosmimi straw cup has made the switch from bottle to sippy cup super simple.

extraction.content:
The Meaning of "Onzenna" Headline: To be a mother is to be “Always.”⁠
 ⁠
 When we chose the name Onzenna, we looked to the Korean word 언제나, which translates to “Always.”⁠
 ⁠
 We chose this because we know that motherhood doesn't have an "off" switch. It isn't a 9-to-5, and it doesn’t end when the baby falls asleep or grows up. It is a constant state of heart—a full-time commitment of care, provision, and love.⁠
 ⁠
 You are a mother in the quiet of 3AM, in the chaos of the afternoon, and in every milestone in between.⁠
 ⁠
 The Evolution : As we transition from K Baby to Onzenna Motherhood Solutions, our mission is matching your commitment. If motherhood is always, then our support should be, too.⁠
 ⁠
 We aren't just here for the "baby stage." We are here for the journey that never ends. We are here for the woman you are becoming, the care you need to sustain yourself, and the solutions that make "always" feel a little more manageable.⁠
 ⁠
 The Promise: Same heart. Deeper mission. Onzenna. Always with you.⁠
 ⁠
 #Onzenna #AlwaysMother #MotherhoodSolutions #FullTimeLove #TheJourneyContinues

analyzation.transcript: If you are a toddler mom, I'm sure you know the struggle of trying to find a good water cup for your kids. Some are super cute, but they leak, or they don't leak, but they aren't super cute, or your kids just don't like them. We've gone through several brands and models to find one that actually works for us. One of our newest additions is this straw cup from Growmimi. Not only is it super cute, but it's also BPA free and very, very durable. I also love that my daughter can operate the lid so that we aren't constantly having to find the cup and open it for her. Now for the ultimate test, is it actually leak proof? Yes, it is. It's even leak proof without the lid on. And I shook this thing around pretty good and not a drop was spilled. So if you're in the market for a new water cup for your toddler, maybe check this brand out. They have lots of super cute designs. The one that I picked out for my daughter is called Rose Coral.

 Just a normal day of balanced meals, snacks, and self-feeding practice. But since we spent the day out at grandma’s, we brought some extra snacks for on-the-go. 
 

 She’s loving these rice puffs from @onzennna_official — the only ingredient is rice. No additives.
 

 They’re perfect for practicing pincer grasp and easy snack moments between meals.

analyzation.transcript: Here's everything that my eight month old ate today. She's teething right now, which means she's extra fussy. My husband was on shift at the fire station, so after her first round of solids, I packed us up and drove just under an hour to my mom's just to have an extra set of hands for a bit. Kept her first meal simple, just foods that I knew she would actually eat. Then later that afternoon, once we got to my mom's, we did snack time. Favorite these pop rice snacks and they've been such an easy option for us lately. The only ingredient is rice, which is why I like them. She loves them. Really light, easy for her to hold, low mess, so they're perfect for when we're out and about. When we got back home, we did dinner. Since she's teething so bad, I really didn't expect her to eat a lot, but she did eat more than I thought she would. But yeah, this is a pretty typical day of what meals look like for her.

Before kids, I never thought about water, but now if we're not hydrated, the whole house feels chaotic. The first thing I do in the morning, I fill up their tumblers because when they're hydrated, moods are better, meltdowns are fewer, and I'm not constantly hearing, I'm thirsty. I love these Grosmimi water bottles because they don't leak. They're stainless steel, so the drinks stay cold for hours, and the soft silicone tip is gentle for little mouths. They've dropped these more times than I can count, and they still hold up. And the best part, they can carry them themselves, which means one less thing for me to juggle. I've learned that water isn't just about hydration, it's about preventing meltdowns and making their little bodies feel better, and making our days a little smoother. Not only are these water bottles super cute and super high quality, but they're made to grow and transition with your child. And as a mom who is always on the go with two very busy toddlers, this has hands down been the best water bottle we've tried. Hydrated kids equal better days and anything that makes our day smoother, I'm keeping.

extraction.content:
Open-cup practice can start earlier than many parents realize 🤍
 

 Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidelines support introducing supervised open-cup practice around 6 months, once solids are introduced. At this age, babies are typically developing the sitting stability and emerging oral motor skills needed to begin learning this new skill.
 

 This cup is labeled for 18 months+, but here I’m demonstrating supported skill exposure, not independent use. Close supervision is always key.
 

 Early exposure helps babies practice:
 • Lip closure
 • Jaw stability
 • Flow control
 • Hand-to-mouth coordination
 

 Over time, this supports smoother transitions away from bottles.
 

 These cups were gifted to me, but all opinions are my own as a pediatric RN + lactation consultant.
 

 Save this for when your baby starts solids 🤍
 

 🫶FOLLOW FOR more breastfeeding and baby care tips 
 

 #FoodgradeStrawCup #StainlessSteelStrawCup #strawcup #Grosmimi #Onzenna

analyzation.transcript: As a lactation consultant and pediatric RN, I love supporting babies as they begin developing new feeding skills, including learning to drink from an open cup. This is a Grosmimi stainless steel tumbler designed to help infants practice early cup skills in a developmentally supportive way. It has measurement markings on the inside, which can be helpful for tracking intake, and a soft silicone rim that's gentle on developing teeth and lips while babies learn flow control. Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidance recommend introducing supervised open cup practice around six months of age, once solids are introduced. At this stage, babies are typically developing the sitting, stability, jaw control, and emerging oral motor skills that are needed to begin practicing safely and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. When practice with close supervision, early exposure can support coordination and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. And one of the best parts for families is that it's completely leakproof, making it practical for everyday use.

extraction.content:
We thought it was food allergies.
 

 Cue the testing. The label reading. The overthinking.
 

 And then we learned… it was eczema.
 

 As both a pediatric clinician and a mom, I know how important the skin barrier is — especially in babies. When that barrier is compromised, flare-ups keep cycling. Bath time used to make me nervous because I didn’t want to make it worse.
 

 After trying what felt like everything, CHA&MOM has been a game changer for her skin.

Open-cup practice can start earlier than many parents realize 🤍
 

 Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidelines support introducing supervised open-cup practice around 6 months, once solids are introduced. At this age, babies are typically developing the sitting stability and emerging oral motor skills needed to begin learning this new skill.
 

 This cup is labeled for 18 months+, but here I’m demonstrating supported skill exposure, not independent use. Close supervision is always key.
 

 Early exposure helps babies practice:
 • Lip closure
 • Jaw stability
 • Flow control
 • Hand-to-mouth coordination
 

 Over time, this supports smoother transitions away from bottles.
 

 These cups were gifted to me, but all opinions are my own as a pediatric RN + lactation consultant.
 

 Save this for when your baby starts solids 🤍
 

 🫶FOLLOW FOR more breastfeeding and baby care tips

It has measurement markings on the inside, which can be helpful for tracking intake, and a soft silicone rim that's gentle on developing teeth and lips while babies learn flow control. Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidance recommend introducing supervised open cup practice around six months of age, once solids are introduced. At this stage, babies are typically developing the sitting, stability, jaw control, and emerging oral motor skills that are needed to begin practicing safely and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. When practice with close supervision, early exposure can support coordination and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. And one of the best parts for families is that it's completely leakproof, making it practical for everyday use.

analyzation.transcript: My baby is almost one, so we're going to start the transition from bottle to cup. Now, he's still taking four bottles a day, one when he wakes up, one after each nap, and then one a little bit before bed. About two weeks ago, we tried to start this process, and then our family came down with the stomach flu, so we just held off. But we tried with this cup, he drank about an ounce and a half, and then when we tried with this one, he took one sip and was over it. We're going to try this cup today. He loves this cup, he drinks his water out of it. He carries this around, so he's familiar with this cup, so I think it might go over a little bit better. And what I'm going to actually do is I'm going to take the straw out, and he can kind of drink it tipped back, almost like it's a bottle. Over the last few weeks, we've been warming his bottle up less and less as time goes on, just so he would get used to drinking cold milk. However, he still definitely prefers it warm, so for today, I'm going to warm up his milk like he used to drink it. I'm also going to keep him in the nursery in his sleep sack in his little warm cozy environment. It was supposed to be a low pressure practice moment, but he actually shocked me and he drank all four ounces from the straw cup.

analyzation.transcript: Watch this. Look how the water comes out slow and gently and not all at once. This is the Gros Mimi stainless steel tumbler. This is a great cup to teach open cup drinking with a gentle flow that gives toddlers time to coordinate their swallow. And the soft silicone tip is kinder on developing teeth and lips. analyzation.transcript: We ditched the bottle at 12 months, and here's how we did it. As a speech language pathologist, I was really mindful about the cup we chose. A lot of leak proof cups have really strong valves, and you end up having to bite the straw to get the milk to come out, and that's not the drinking pattern we want to reinforce. That's why we love the Grosmimi straw cup. At first, I took the straw out, so he could tip it back and drink it like it was a bottle. Once he was familiar with it, I put the straw back in, and he was able to drink it just like normal from a straw cup. Comment milk, and I'll send you a link to my favorite cup.

analyzation.transcript: When the twins were newborns, they developed eczema and baby acne. And seeing that made me realize just how delicate their skin really is. And I know that baby acne is mostly caused by hormones passed from mom to birth, but it's also caused by environmental factors as well. And as an esthetician and a twin mom, that experience made me extremely picky. I knew I wanted only unscented products, the most gentle ingredients, and formulas that actually support a healthy skin barrier. So I spent a long time researching and comparing baby skincare before I felt comfortable committing to anything. And that's when I found Cha and Mom. This is a brand crafted by Cha Hospital. It's Korea's number one OBGYN center with over 60 years of expertise caring for moms and babies. What really stood out to me is their phyto-saling name, which helps support the skin in a way similar to the natural microbiome babies receive at birth from their moms. The P. S. lotion is now part of our daily bath routine, and the P. S. cream is what I reach for when their skin needs extra comfort. And as a mom and a skincare specialist, this is the one I can really trust for my boys.

Open-cup practice can start earlier than many parents realize 🤍
 

 Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidelines support introducing supervised open-cup practice around 6 months, once solids are introduced. At this age, babies are typically developing the sitting stability and emerging oral motor skills needed to begin learning this new skill.
 

 This cup is labeled for 18 months+, but here I’m demonstrating supported skill exposure, not independent use. Close supervision is always key.
 

 Early exposure helps babies practice:
 • Lip closure
 • Jaw stability
 • Flow control
 • Hand-to-mouth coordination
 

 Over time, this supports smoother transitions away from bottles.
 

 These cups were gifted to me, but all opinions are my own as a pediatric RN + lactation consultant.
 

 Save this for when your baby starts solids 🤍
 

 🫶FOLLOW FOR more breastfeeding and baby care tips

analyzation.transcript: As a lactation consultant and pediatric RN, I love supporting babies as they begin developing new feeding skills, including learning to drink from an open cup. This is a Grosmimi stainless steel tumbler designed to help infants practice early cup skills in a developmentally supportive way. It has measurement markings on the inside, which can be helpful for tracking intake, and a soft silicone rim that's gentle on developing teeth and lips while babies learn flow control. Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidance recommend introducing supervised open cup practice around six months of age, once solids are introduced. At this stage, babies are typically developing the sitting, stability, jaw control, and emerging oral motor skills that are needed to begin practicing safely and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. When practice with close supervision, early exposure can support coordination and make the transition away from bottles smoother over time. And one of the best parts for families is that it's completely leakproof, making it practical for everyday use.

Open-cup practice can start earlier than many parents realize 🤍
 

 Evidence-based pediatric feeding guidelines support introducing supervised open-cup practice around 6 months, once solids are introduced. At this age, babies are typically developing the sitting stability and emerging oral motor skills needed to begin learning this new skill.
 

 This cup is labeled for 18 months+, but here I’m demonstrating supported skill exposure, not independent use. Close supervision is always key.
 

 Early exposure helps babies practice:
 • Lip closure
 • Jaw stability
 • Flow control
 • Hand-to-mouth coordination
 

 Over time, this supports smoother transitions away from bottles.
 

 These cups were gifted to me, but all opinions are my own as a pediatric RN + lactation consultant.
 

 Save this for when your baby starts solids 🤍
 

 🫶FOLLOW FOR more breastfeeding and baby care tips

The Meaning of "Onzenna" Headline: To be a mother is to be “Always.”⁠
 ⁠
 When we chose the name Onzenna, we looked to the Korean word 언제나, which translates to “Always.”⁠
 ⁠
 We chose this because we know that motherhood doesn't have an "off" switch. It isn't a 9-to-5, and it doesn’t end when the baby falls asleep or grows up. It is a constant state of heart—a full-time commitment of care, provision, and love.⁠
 ⁠
 You are a mother in the quiet of 3AM, in the chaos of the afternoon, and in every milestone in between.⁠
 ⁠
 The Evolution : As we transition from K Baby to Onzenna Motherhood Solutions, our mission is matching your commitment. If motherhood is always, then our support should be, too.⁠
 ⁠
 We aren't just here for the "baby stage." We are here for the journey that never ends. We are here for the woman you are becoming, the care you need to sustain yourself, and the solutions that make "always" feel a little more manageable.⁠
 ⁠
 The Promise: Same heart. Deeper mission. Onzenna. Always with you.⁠
 ⁠
 #Onzenna #AlwaysMother #MotherhoodSolutions #FullTimeLove #TheJourneyContinues

extraction.content:
˗ˏˋ ステンレスマグはこれに決まり ˎˊ˗
 

 こんばんは、しろです🌿
 見ていただきありがとうございます☺️
 

 今回は大人気の韓国ブランド
 @grosmimi_japan さんの
 ステンレスマグをご紹介します🧸
 

 歩けるようになって外遊びが増えると、
 ◽️夏は冷たい飲み物
 ◽️冬はあったかい飲み物
 どっちも持ち歩けるマグが欲しくなって…
 

 そんな時に出会ったのが
 見た目も機能性も大満足なこのマグ🤍
 正直、ママのテンションかなり上がりました🥹
 

 【推しポイント】
 

 ✔ ストローの先が“バッテン構造”
 振り回しても、倒しても
 中身がこぼれにくい設計◎
 

 ✔ パーツがシンプルで洗いやすい
 ストローが太めで
 汚れがたまりにくく、衛生的✨
 煮沸・電子レンジ・食洗機OKなのも嬉しいポイント
 

 ✔ 医療レベルのステンレス素材
 ニオイ移りしにくく、
 飲み物の味が変わらないのも安心
 

 ✔ 保温・保冷どちらも約6時間キープ
 季節を問わず年中使えるから
 1本あるとほんと便利👏🏻
 

 ✔ 見た目がとにかく可愛い
 200ml / 300mlの2サイズ展開で
 用途に合わせて選べるよ🫶🏻
 

 このステンレスマグは
 **対象年齢12ヶ月〜**ですが、
 同じシリーズで
 6ヶ月以降から使えるマグもあるので
 月齢に合わせてチェックしてみてね👶🏻
 

 詳しい情報は
 プロフィールのリンクから
 【ストローマグ】で検索🔍
 

 ーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーー
 

 1歳👦🏻育児中ママが
 育児がちょっとラクになる
 グッズと裏ワザ発信中🌿
 

 フォローして
 他の投稿ものぞいてみてね🤍

 #ストローマグ

author.nickname: もん|ママがちょっとラクになる育児
extraction.content:
ぜひ他の投稿も見てってね🌟
 @mon_yuru.ikuji
 

 こんばんは、もんです🧡
 

 年末に紹介した可愛い韓国のストローマグ🧸💓
 娘っちも気に入ってて毎日使ってる🥤
 

 でもまじで後悔してる…
 一年前から知りたかった！
 生まれた時から知りたかった！
 なんなら1人目から知りたかったーーー😭💓
 

 まずね！
 今手放せないのはカミカミ期で他のストローを噛みちぎってしまった娘っち👶🏻
 でもこのストローはめっちゃ噛んでるのに耐えてる🥹❤️‍🔥
 すごい！！
 噛んで伸ばす娘っち見て、ママ友もビックリしてた🤣
 

 そしてそして…
 知らなかったことを後悔した理由が、これピジョンのニップルにジャストフィットなのー🥺
 0歳のミルクでも哺乳瓶代わりに使える🍼
 ステンレスなら冬のお出かけの時とか重宝するし、
 プラもニップル〜ストロー①〜ストロー②って段階踏んだらめっちゃ長く使える！
 

 お気に入りを長く使えるの最高じゃんね🥹💓
 楽天ルームに載せてるからチェックしてねー🔎
 

 フォローして他の投稿も見てってねー🌟
 @mon_yuru.ikuji
 

 *¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨*
 いつもありがとう🫶
 年間350日ほどをワンオペで👶1歳👦3歳を自宅保育中🏠🦖
 笑って過ごすために！すこしでも余裕を生むために！時短技や楽できるグッズなど発信中🦥
 

 少しずつ手抜きして、時間貯金✨
 自分時間つくろ❤️‍🔥
 *¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨¨*

extraction.content:
@emachi_mom 🎁応募方法は最後に…⇣
 

 こんばんは！えまちです🧸
 今日もとってもお疲れ様です🌙
 

 今日の投稿は
 韓国で大人気のストローマグ🥤
 

 年末に日本デビューしたばかりの
 @grosmimi_japanさん
 

 実は…まぴが使ったマグは
 これで５つ目…！
 なんだけどこれが今一番お気に入りで
 「恐竜がいい！恐竜にする！🦖」って言ってる🤭
 

 動画で紹介した以外の
 お気に入りポイントは…！
 

 ◇マグのボトルの耐熱が200℃まで
 ◇電子レンジOK
 ◇食洗機対応
 ◇変色、変形、ニオイ残りがない
 とかも他のマグと違うよね😉🥤ˎˊ˗
 

 そして
 👶🏻12ヶ月(1歳)のお子ちゃんから使用OK
 

 って嬉しいポイントたくさんの
 ワンタッチ式ストローマグ 300mlを
 今回はなんと…！！
 

 グロミミさんにご協力頂いて
 大好きなフォロワーさんに感謝企画！！
 

 合計２名様に🎁するよ！♡
  🦖恐竜 １名様
  🦄ペガサス １名様
 

 【応募方法】
 ❶私をフォロー (@emachi_mom)
 ❷グロミミ公式アカウントをフォロー (@grosmimi_japan)
 ❸この動画に、ほしい柄をコメント
 ❹私から♥️で応募完了です
 

 【応募期間】
 本日〜2/7(土) 23:59
 

 【当選発表】
 2/11(水祝) ストーリーズで発表📢ˎˊ˗
  ⇢ハイライトに残します！
 ⚠️当選発表時にフォロー解除されていた方は
  抽選の対象外となります
 

 ※念のため、当選したフォロワーさんには
  私から当選DMを送ります☺️💌
 

 【発送】
 当選者確定後に、グロミミさん(@grosmimi_japan)から
 直接DMでご案内、順次発送になります💌ˎˊ˗
 

 それでは、たくさんのご応募
 楽しみにお待ちしています🥰📝
 

 本当に素敵な商品なので
 大切なフォロワーさんに届くことが
 なによりとっても嬉しい！！！♡
 

 いつも私の投稿やストーリーを見てくれて
 温かいメッセージを本当にありがとう☺️♡
 

 - - - - - - - - — 𖤘 - - - - - - - - —𖤘 - - - - - — - - -
 

 ２歳の男の子をゆるっと育てるママ、えまちです🧸
 @emachi_mom ←フォローして他の投稿もみてね🤍
 

 ◇ 明日やりたくなる育児の裏ワザ
 ◇ リサーチ大好きママが推す
  トキめくシンプル可愛い便利グッズ
 ◇ 保育園生活と時短復帰のリアル、
  正直レポはストーリー限定🕊️
 

 近くに頼れる人がいなくても
 がんばりすぎなくて大丈夫。１人じゃないよ。
 一緒に喜んで悩んで、子育て楽しもう〜🤝🏻🤍

analyzation.transcript: よく見ててね。え、こぼれてないってなるストローマグが最高すぎた。最後にプレゼントあるよ。ストローマグって意外とこぼれん。このマグ実はここに十字の切れ込みが入ってるから逆さまにしても全く漏れてこんの。すごくない？しかもここが軽い力で押せるから息子も1人で開けられちゃうし、お茶が少なくなったらマグを回せば最後まで飲みやすいのも子供思いすぎる。これ本体が煮沸消毒できる特殊素材だから丈夫で長く使えそうってのも嬉しいよね。そしてなんと今回このワンタッチ式ストローマグをフォロワーさん限定でそれぞれお1人ずつ計2名様にプレゼントするよ。応募方法は本文見てね。

extraction.content:
多くのママ・パパは、ストローマグは「飲むためだけのもの」と思っています。
 でも、小さな積み重ねが大きな発達につながるんです🌱
 

 今日からできる、3つのポイントはこちら👇
 

 ① 飲む前にしっかり「唇を閉じる」
 → 口周りの筋肉を使い、発語の土台づくりをサポート
 

 ② まずは自分で選ばせてみる
 → 小さな選択が、決定力や実行機能の発達につながる
 

 ③ 自分でフタを閉めてもらう
 → 自己調整力・自立心・手先の発達を育む
 

 ただのマグではありません。
 毎日の発達習慣が大事です✨
 

 保存して、今日からぜひ試してみてくださいね 💛

extraction.content:
˗ˏˋ 赤ちゃんのステンレスマグはこれˎˊ˗
 

 こんばんは、しろです🌿
 見ていただきありがとうございます☺️
 

 ステンレスタイプのマグ
 探してない？👀
 

 今回は大人気の韓国ブランド
 @grosmimi_japan さんの
 ステンレスマグをご紹介します🧸
 

 歩けるようになって外遊びが増えると、
 ◽️夏は冷たい飲み物
 ◽️冬はあったかい飲み物
 どっちも持ち歩けるマグが欲しくなって…
 

 そんな時に出会ったのが
 見た目も機能性も大満足なこのマグ🤍
 正直、ママのテンションかなり上がりました🥹
 

 【推しポイント】
 

 ✔ ストローの先が“バッテン構造”
 振り回しても、倒しても
 中身がこぼれにくい設計◎

 ストローが太めで

 ✔ 医療レベルのステンレス素材
 ニオイ移りしにくく、
 飲み物の味が変わらないのも安心
 

 ✔ 保温・保冷どちらも約6時間キープ
 季節を問わず年中使えるから
 1本あるとほんと便利👏🏻
 

 ✔ 見た目がとにかく可愛い
 200ml / 300mlの2サイズ展開で
 用途に合わせて選べるよ🫶🏻
 

 このステンレスマグは
 **対象年齢12ヶ月〜**ですが、
 同じシリーズで
 6ヶ月以降から使えるマグもあるので
 月齢に合わせてチェックしてみてね👶🏻
 

 詳しい情報は
 プロフィールのリンクから
 【ストローマグ】で検索🔍
 

 ーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーーー
 

 1歳👦🏻育児中ママが

@emachi_mom 🎁企画で大人気だった
 

 こんばんは！えまちです🧸

 今日の投稿は
 韓国で大人気のストローマグ🥤

 @grosmimi_japanさん

 たくさん応募してくださって
 本当にありがとうございました！！♡
 

 今回動画で紹介するのは
 ステンレスバージョンだよ☺️

 気温が上がってくると

 初夏〜秋まではステンレス１本あった方が
 お外のおでかけに安心☺️
 これは去年一昨年と痛感してる。
 

 @grosmimi_japanさんのマグは
 ストロー部分がやわらかくて
 十字の切れ込みのおかげで、本当に漏れにくい！！
 しかもフタのロックが

 - - - - - - - - — 𖤘 - - - - - - - - —𖤘 - - - - - — - - -
 

 ２歳の男の子をゆるっと育てるママ、えまちです🧸
 @emachi_mom ←フォローして他の投稿もみてね🤍
 

 ◇ 明日やりたくなる育児の裏ワザ

  トキめくシンプル可愛い便利グッズ
 ◇ 保育園生活と時短復帰のリアル、
  正直レポはストーリー限定🕊️
 

 近くに頼れる人がいなくても
 がんばりすぎなくて大丈夫。１人じゃないよ。
 一緒に喜んで悩んで、子育て楽しもう〜🤝🏻🤍

analyzation.transcript: え、何これ、全然こぼれん。韓国初のストローマグが使いやすすぎたからちょっと聞いて。ストローマグって意外とこぼれん？このマグ実はここに十字の切れ込みが入ってるから、逆さまにしても全く漏れてこんの。すごくない？しかもここが押しやすいから息子1人で開けられて好きな時に飲めるのも嬉しい。更にこれ見た目が可愛いだけじゃなくてステンレス素材が医療グレードのつわもの。温保冷が6時間もできるから夏の麦茶腐りやすい問題もこれで安心。しかも錆びにくいなんてほんとママ思い。

author.nickname: めめこ⌇ ムリなく楽しむ0歳育児
extraction.content:
【ストローマグのデビューにぴったり.ᐟ】
 ⁡
 ご覧いただき、ありがとうございます♡
 8か月の女の子を育ててます、めめこです⋈*.
 

 今回は韓国で国民的人気ブランド
 @grosmimi_japan さんの
 ストローマグをご紹介します🩶
 

 なんと.ᐟ日本初上陸なんです🙌✨
 

 そろそろストローもマグもデビューしたいけど、
 どんなものを選べばいいか迷っちゃうよね🥺
 

 例えば...
 ◻︎ストローを吸う感覚つかめる？飲める？
 ◻︎漏れたりしない？
 ◻︎逆流しない？
 ◻︎洗うパーツ多いと面倒...
 こんなことわたしは考えてたんだけど、
 

 このマグならまるっと解決してくれます🪄︎︎⟡.·
 

 【ここが推せる.ᐟ.ᐟ.ᐟ】
 

 ︎ ☑︎やわらかい素材のストロー
  自然とストロー飲みの練習が出来ちゃう🥤
 

 ︎ ☑︎持ちやすい360°回転ハンドル
  グリップが握りやすくて自分で飲む力が育つ🌱
 

 ︎ ☑︎“クロスカット”ストロー
  逆さにして振っても、勢いよく倒しても
  漏れにくい構造で逆流もしにくい👏
 

 ︎ ☑︎簡単に全パーツが取り外せるからお手入れがラク
  ストローも太くて洗いやすく、取り付けも簡単.ᐟ
  煮沸・食洗機・電子レンジと全てOK👌´-
 

 ︎ ☑︎医療グレード素材“PPSU本体”
  熱や衝撃に強いからマイクロプラスチックが
  発生しにくく、赤ちゃんにも安心👶🏻
 

 わたしは、
 ✧洗練されたデザインとオシャレな色味
 ✧PPSU本体だから透明感を保ちやすい
 この2つもお気に入りポイントだよ♡
 

 初めてのストローマグはもうコレに決まり✨
 

 詳しい情報は
 楽天ROOMで🔎【グロミミ】って検索してね.ᐟ
 

 ‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋‎﹋
 保育士ママのめめこが
 育児のちょっと困ったを解決する
 裏ワザやグッズを発信中🪄
 こんなにも愛おしい赤ちゃんの時期を
 ゆったりと楽しめる毎日を送ろう🪽
 ⁡
 フォロー、いいね♡、コメント
 とってもうれしいです☺️❤️
 気軽にDMでお喋りしましょー.ᐟ

analyzation.transcript: 何これすごい。初めてのストローマグはこれが正解だった。離乳食にも慣れてきてそろそろストローの練習しなきゃってマグを探してたんだけど、正直ストローの練習ってうまく進められるか不安だった。そんな時に見つけたこれ。ストローが柔らかいから自然と練習できるし、360度回転の持ちやすいグリップだから赤ちゃん1人でも持てちゃう。さらにすごいのが赤ちゃんがブンブンしてもガンガンして落としても中身がこぼれる心配ないし衝撃にも強い。実はこの素材、医療機器や飛行機の窓にも使われてて、赤ちゃんにとっても安心素材。そして何たってパーツの取り外しが簡単で洗いやすいのもポイント。お手入れが楽なのはママにはありがたいよね。こんなにおしゃれな見た目なのに機能も安心も揃ってる。グロミミが気になる人は本文チェックしてね。

analyzation.transcript: え、こんなに振っても全然漏れへん。韓国で大人気のマグが運転中ママの救世主やった。喉渇いたってぐずられるの、あるあるやない？これに変えてから2歳の娘でも簡単に開けられて、1人で飲んでくれるようになったから、ぐずられることもなくなったし、ブンブン振っても漏れんから運転に集中できるようになった。これ保温効果もあって、最大6時間キープしてくれるし、取っ手が360度回るから持ちやすいのも推しポイント。今まで水こぼされるたびにイライラしちょったけど、このマグは気に入ってくれてちゃんと飲んでくれた。運転中のストレス今日で卒業しよ。楽天ルームでグロミミって検索してね。
